entry.title: Re: Not able to view the AI units cosumed under FInance &...
entry.content:
Regarding to your issue, please refer to KBA 3567298 - Inability to View AI Unit Consumption in Business AI application - SAP for Me 

Abstract below information for your reference:

The Business AI application in SAP for Me will only show entries if using SAP AI Unit SKU 8019164. 

Old AI entitlements not displayed: 

AI entitlements purchased or signed in between October 5, 2023, and August 4, 2024, are not displayed on the SAP for Me Business AI card. Unfortunately, these older purchased Units SKUs cannot be shown. Please contact your SAP Account Manager as per 2626344 or the SAP Order Management as per 1660069. The previous purchase needs to be renewed and booked again for the AI Units to become visible in SAP for Me.

Thus please reach out your Account Manager as following to ask for help

entry.title: "Your AI kitchen is ready. Here's why you're not cooking yet."
entry.content:
Introduction

Think of your AI journey like building a restaurant. You don't just open the doors and start cooking. There is a process. And every organization that skipped that process learned the hard way — usually in front of a paying customer.

If you are working with SAP Customer Experience solutions — Commerce, Sales Cloud, Service Cloud, or SAP Engagement Cloud — you have probably already heard that AI is built right into the platform. And that is true. The capabilities are there. Joule is ready. Embedded AI is waiting to be switched on. But here is what nobody tells you clearly enough: having the kitchen does not mean you are ready to cook.

"This isn't twelve random services. It's one journey with four stages. You start at Discover, and every service has a clear reason to exist in that sequence."

In this blog post, you will walk through the twelve AI services that make up the SAP LOB Advanced Success Plan AI Service Catalog — what they are, why they are sequenced the way they are, and what they specifically mean for your SAP CX investment. The restaurant analogy will guide you the whole way, because once you see it, you cannot unsee it.

Background

The state of AI in SAP Customer Experience

SAP Customer Experience is not short on AI. Product recommendations in Commerce. Intelligent case routing in Service Cloud. Predictive lead scoring in Sales Cloud. AI-generated audience segments in Marketing. And Joule — SAP's generative AI copilot — running across all of them.

The honest conversation that needs to happen more often is this: many customers have already licensed these capabilities and have not yet turned them on. Not because they do not want to. Because they do not know where to start, or they started in the wrong place and it did not go well.

Why AI adoption keeps failing — three patterns worth knowing

Across enterprise CX deployments, the same three failure patterns appear again and again:

  • Starting blind. Activating AI without business context and Process Knowledge  — leading to duplicated effort and missed quick wins.
  • Skipping governance. Deploying tools without putting governance, ownership, or trust guardrails in place first — then being surprised when AI outputs cannot be audited or explained.
  • Forgetting the humans. Going live technically while the people who are supposed to use the AI were never prepared — and then measuring low adoption rates six months later and blaming the technology.

None of these are technology problems. They are journey problems. And that is exactly what the SAP CX AI Service Catalog was designed to solve.

Four phases. Twelve services. One kitchen.

The framework is built around four phases and twelve services. Each phase has a clear purpose. Each service has a clear reason to exist in its position in the sequence. Here is how it all maps to the restaurant analogy — and to your SAP CX reality.

Phase 1 — DISCOVER: First, understand the kitchen

"What ingredients do you have? What's already on the menu? That's AI Foundation Orientation."

Service 1 — AI Foundation Orientation

"Understand the kitchen, the ingredients, and what is already on the menu."

Before you plan a single AI initiative, you need to know your starting point. This service gives your team a structured level-set on SAP Business AI, the AI Foundation architecture, and Joule — so that every decision made after this point is grounded in what you actually have, not what you think you have.

Service 2 — AI Use Case Navigator

"Pick the dishes customers actually want before stocking the premium ingredients."

A restaurant that tries to serve everything has a menu nobody can read. This service uses live demos of SAP CX AI scenarios and a facilitated prioritization process to produce a shortlist of high-value use cases specific to your business — whether that is AI-assisted case resolution, next-best-offer in Commerce, or intelligent lead scoring in Sales Cloud. You invest in what your customers will actually value.

Phase 2 — PLAN/EXPLORE: Set the kitchen up to run well

"And you set up food safety — AI Trust & Compliance. Not exciting, but try running a restaurant without it."

Service 3 — AI Trust & Compliance

"Just like food safety keeps a restaurant trustworthy, AI governance keeps AI safe, compliant, and reliable."

AI that interacts with your customers carries real risk: biased recommendations, privacy violations, responses that cannot be explained or audited. This service establishes the guardrails — GDPR compliance, model transparency, output auditability — before a single customer sees an AI-generated result. Not optional. Not something to revisit after go-live.

Service 4 — AI CoE Foundation

"Build the head-chef team, kitchen rules, and recipe governance."

The best kitchen in the world fails without clear ownership and a chain of command. AI CoE Foundation helps your organization build the internal Center of Excellence — the team structure, governance model, and capability-building framework that means AI adoption does not collapse when the external experts leave.

Service 5 — AI Change Enablement

"Train the staff so the smart kitchen runs smoothly."

Your service agents, sales reps, and marketing managers are the ones who will use the AI every day. If they do not trust it, do not understand it, or do not know when to override it, adoption will stall regardless of how well the technology was deployed. This service prepares your people before go-live — not after the adoption numbers come in disappointing.

Phase 3 — REALIZE: Now you cook

"Now you cook. Joule Activation gets the core system connected. AI Agent Activation gives the digital sous-chefs their jobs. Embedded AI blends intelligence directly into the process — like seasoning in the sauce."

Five services. The kitchen powers on.

Service 6 — AI Activation Readiness Session

"Make sure the kitchen, tools, and suppliers are ready before opening."

A pre-deployment technical check confirming data quality, integration health, and configuration readiness across your in-scope CX environment. This is where you catch problems before they become customer-facing incidents — not after.

Service 7 — Joule Activation Session

"Start connecting the core systems and activate it."

The moment Joule powers on across your CX tools. Platform integration, user provisioning, and core activation — done correctly from day one. Your service agents, sales team, and commerce managers start interacting with AI where they already work.

Service 8 — Embedded AI Activation

"Blend AI directly into the cooking process, like seasoning in the sauce."

Product recommendations. Churn scores. Case routing. Sentiment-triggered escalation. These run invisibly inside your CX processes — but only if each scenario is correctly configured and validated. This service confirms every in-scope embedded AI capability is producing reliable outputs before it touches a real customer interaction.

Service 9 — AI Agent Activation

"Give the digital sous-chefs clear jobs and proper supervision. One at a time."

AI agents handle real customer interactions — resolving tickets, guiding self-service journeys. This service activates them with defined scope, oversight controls, and human-handoff protocols. Sequenced deliberately: one agent at a time, until each one is running with precision.

Phase 4 — RUN/OPERATE: Review, optimize, expand

"And then you keep optimizing. What's selling? What's not? Where do you expand? That's AI Operational Excellence."

Service 10 — AI Process Fit-Gap Analysis

"See where the standard menu works and where you need a custom special."

Not every customer's CX process maps cleanly to SAP's standard AI capabilities. This service identifies exactly where the gaps are and recommends whether the answer is configuration, extension, or a custom build — preventing months of effort spent on the wrong solution.

Service 11 — AI Operational Excellence

"Review what's selling, what's working, and where to expand the franchise."

The restaurant is open. Now the work is continuous improvement. This service reviews AI adoption metrics and value realization against original targets — is case deflection in Service Cloud hitting the forecast? Are Commerce recommendations lifting basket value? — and builds the roadmap for the next wave of AI investment.

Service 12 — Joule Studio Build

"Create custom signature dishes when the standard menu is not enough."

When SAP's out-of-the-box AI does not fully address a specific CX scenario, this service provides guided support to develop custom AI extensions — responsibly, within clean core principles, so your system stays upgradable while your AI stays differentiated.

Running throughout all four phases: Success Expert Services

Service Planning, Adoption Checkpoint, and Innovation Checkpoint run continuously across the entire journey — not as occasional project check-ins, but as the coordination layer that ensures every service lands in the right sequence, adoption milestones are being met alongside technical progress, and your team is always informed about what is new in SAP CX AI.

Think of them as the maître d' of your AI journey. Always present. Never optional.

 Results — what the journey actually delivers

Here is what customers who follow this sequence consistently experience — phase by phase:

After Discover:

  • A shared, documented picture of your current AI estate — no more guessing what is active.
  • A prioritized use case shortlist that business and IT have both agreed on.
  • Alignment between teams on where the journey starts and why.

After Plan/Explore:

  • A governance structure and CoE that will outlast the project.
  • A compliance framework that satisfies audit requirements before go-live.
  • A trained, AI-ready user population — prepared before the technology arrives.

After Realize:

  • Joule activated and in use across the CX tools your teams already work in.
  • Embedded AI scenarios validated against real data before touching a customer interaction.
  • AI agents running with defined scope, oversight, and human-handoff protocols.
  • Gaps between standard capability and process requirements documented and resolved.

After Run/Operate:

  • Closed-loop value measurement — you know what your AI investment actually delivered.
  • A data-backed roadmap for the next wave of AI in your CX landscape.
  • Custom capabilities where needed, built safely within clean core principles.

The difference between organizations that reach sustained AI value and those that do not is rarely the quality of the technology. It is almost always the quality of the journey around it.

 Conclusion and what comes next

This is not twelve random services. It is one journey with four stages. You start at Discover, and every service has a clear reason to exist in that sequence.

The restaurant analogy works because the adoption challenge is the same: you cannot serve a great meal without first knowing your kitchen, picking the right menu, training your team, and then continuously reviewing what is working. Organizations that treat AI adoption as this kind of structured journey — from Foundation Orientation through to Operational Excellence — reach value faster, sustain adoption longer, and build the organizational capability to scale AI across their entire customer experience.

Where the journey goes next

The field is evolving quickly. A few directions worth watching as this methodology matures:

  • Agentic AI at scale — as multi-agent orchestration matures in SAP CX, the framework will need to govern agent-to-agent interactions across Commerce, Sales, and Service simultaneously.
  • Industry-specific tracks — future iterations of this catalog should develop industry-specific variants for retail, utilities, and financial services CX scenarios.
  • Value measurement standards — standardized benchmarks for CX AI value — deflection rates, basket lift, pipeline conversion — will allow organizations to evaluate outcomes against a curated reference dataset rather than self-defined metrics.
  • Customer-led innovation pathways — formal routes for customer-originated CX AI use cases to be evaluated and contributed back into SAP's standard capability roadmap.

Great AI. Even better service.

Sustained AI value is built on trust, adoption, and continuous improvement

Where are you on this journey? Have you already started with AI Foundation Orientation, or are you still figuring out where to begin? I would love to hear what is working for your team and what questions are still open.

Share your thoughts in the comments below — and if you found this post useful, explore the SAP Customer Experience topic page on SAP Community for more expert content on AI adoption, Joule, and the Advanced Success Plan.

entry.title: Duplicate Tax Number Error During Business Partner Mass Change
entry.content:
I performed a mass change of Business Partner names using the Mass Change Business Partner application to update supplier names in accordance with the agreed naming convention.

The import file contains Business Partners and only updates the following field:

  • Table: BUT000 – General
  • Field: NAME_ORG1 – Name 1

The import file does not contain or modify any tax identification number information.

However, during the validation step, the system displays the following error message:

Another business partner already has this tax number.

Please investigate why the duplicate tax number check is triggered even though the mass change only updates the Business Partner name and does not modify any tax number data.

entry.title: Re: UD Reversal at Warehouse
entry.content:
Hi customer,

Thank you for your question.

It is not possible to reverse usage decision stock posting for EWM inspection lot (origin:17). Although for non-EWM inspection lots, this can be done by the modification solution included in SAP Note 175842 (report RQEVAC50), it cannot be used in EWM environment. This is because in case of EWM inspection lots, all physical movement occurs in EWM via the follow-up action of the usage decision, which is not reversible.

However, KBA 2551912 describes approaches for adjusting the stock at EWM side.

 

Best regards,
Lily

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Safekeeper 2022 Is Here: What It Means for Your Upgrade Journey
entry.content:
Announcing the Release of SAP S/4HANA Safekeeper 2022

As outlined in SAP Note 3424346, SAP has released SAP S/4HANA Safekeeper 2022.

Although this is not a new SAP product, it represents an important milestone for customers running SAP S/4HANA 2022. Safekeeper is released for specific SAP S/4HANA innovation releases to help eligible customers continue operating their existing release after the end of mainstream maintenance, while they plan and execute an upgrade to a newer release under mainstream maintenance.

The availability of SAP S/4HANA Safekeeper 2022 gives customers additional flexibility to remain on SAP S/4HANA 2022 for a limited period while preparing their transition to a more recent SAP S/4HANA release.

Why is there a three-year gap before the next Safekeeper release?

One question customers may notice is why the next planned Safekeeper release—for SAP S/4HANA 2023—is expected only three years later.

The answer lies in SAP's updated SAP S/4HANA release strategy, introduced in 2022.

Beginning with SAP S/4HANA 2023, SAP implemented several significant lifecycle improvements:

  • A two-year innovation release cycle, replacing the previous annual cadence.

  • Seven years of mainstream maintenance for each innovation release, extended from the previous five-year period.

  • Feature Package Stacks (FPS) that deliver continuous innovation in smaller, easier-to-adopt increments without requiring customers to move immediately to the next innovation release.

These changes provide customers with longer planning horizons, greater operational stability, and more predictable upgrade cycles. Consequently, lifecycle milestones—including future Safekeeper releases—are now naturally spaced further apart.

What does this mean for SAP S/4HANA 2022 customers?

For organizations currently running SAP S/4HANA 2022, the release of SAP S/4HANA Safekeeper 2022 provides additional flexibility when planning their application lifecycle.

Customers who need more time beyond mainstream maintenance to complete testing, business validation, or transformation initiatives can leverage Safekeeper (subject to the applicable commercial terms and eligibility criteria) while preparing for their upgrade.

However, Safekeeper should be viewed as a bridge—not a destination. Customers are encouraged to define their upgrade roadmap early so they can benefit from the innovations, security enhancements, and longer maintenance horizons available in newer SAP S/4HANA releases.

Why SAP S/4HANA 2023 and 2025 deserve serious consideration

Customers upgrading from SAP S/4HANA 2022 will almost certainly target either SAP S/4HANA 2023 or SAP S/4HANA 2025.

These releases deliver far more than a longer maintenance timeline.

SAP S/4HANA 2023: The beginning of SAP's next-generation ERP lifecycle

SAP S/4HANA 2023 is the first release built on SAP's new lifecycle strategy, providing seven years of mainstream maintenance and a more predictable two-year innovation cadence.

It also marks the beginning of the Business AI era for SAP S/4HANA. For eligible SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition deployments, SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 introduces support for Joule, SAP's generative AI copilot. Users can interact with business applications using natural language, receive contextual assistance, and streamline navigation across business processes.

Combined with continuous innovation delivered through Feature Package Stacks, SAP S/4HANA 2023 establishes a strong long-term platform for customers modernizing their ERP landscape.

SAP S/4HANA 2025: Building on the foundation

SAP S/4HANA 2025 builds on the 2023 foundation with broader Business AI capabilities and further enhancements across the application.

Highlights include:

  • Expanded Joule integration and additional AI-powered business scenarios.

  • Enhanced Fiori user experience with refined applications and embedded insights.

  • Stronger Clean Core support through additional released APIs, business events, and extensibility options.

  • Functional enhancements across Finance, Supply Chain, Manufacturing, Asset Management, Sustainability, and other business areas.

  • A mainstream maintenance horizon extending to approximately 2032, providing customers with an even longer period of lifecycle stability.

For organizations beginning a new implementation or planning a major upgrade over the next few years, SAP S/4HANA 2025 is likely to be the preferred target release, balancing innovation, stability, and long-term support.

Final thoughts

The release of SAP S/4HANA Safekeeper 2022 provides customers with valuable flexibility, allowing additional time to prepare for their next SAP S/4HANA upgrade where needed.

At the same time, it reinforces the importance of proactive lifecycle planning. Whether the destination is SAP S/4HANA 2023 or SAP S/4HANA 2025, customers have an opportunity to move beyond simply remaining supported and instead take advantage of SAP's latest innovations in Business AI, Clean Core, user experience, and continuous functional enhancements.

Safekeeper helps extend the runway—but the real value lies in reaching the next generation of SAP S/4HANA.

entry.title: SAP Build Code BAS -/ui5-create-app fails with "@ui5/mcp-server" error and Accept Application button
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am trying to create a freestyle SAPUI5 application in SAP Build Code (BAS) using the new Joule command:
/ui5-create-app

Prompt used

/ui5-create-app Create a freestyle SAPUI5 application for the Manage Sales Order CAP service.
Use the OData V4 service:
/service/manage_Sales_Order_App

CAP Project Information
I already have a CAP project generated successfully using Joule.
Project structure:
db/
srv/
app/
The OData service is available and generated correctly.

Joule Response
Joule returns the following proposal:
Let's configure your Manage Sales Order app using JavaScript instead of TypeScript.
Namespace: com.myorg.managesalesorder
Base Path:
/home/user/projects/Manage_Sales_Order_App/app
Create App Directory: true
OData V4 URL:
service/manage_Sales_Order_App/
OData Entity Set:
SalesOrders
TypeScript: false
Once you're ready, you can accept the application proposal
by pressing the "Accept Application" button.
Issue
The "Accept Application" button never appears.

At the same time, I can see the following error in the BAS Error panel:
Failed to generate app proposal:

Cannot find package '@ui5/mcp-server'
imported from

/extbin/local/openvscode-server/extensions/
sap-ux-help-extension-1.28.0/dist/extension-min.cjs

Did you mean to import
"@ui5/mcp-server/lib/api.js"?

Source:
SAP Fiori Tools - Guided Development

Troubleshooting Performed
I have already tried:

Refreshing BAS browser window
Opening a new Joule conversation
Using a simplified /ui5-create-app prompt
Creating a new Development Space
Reopening the project

The same error occurs every time.

Environment
SAP Build Code / SAP Business Application Studio
SAP Fiori Tools - Guided Development
Extension version reported in error:
sap-ux-help-extension-1.28.0
Questions
Is this a known issue with the SAP Fiori Tools / UI5 Create Application assistant?
Has anyone encountered the missing package error:
@ui5/mcp-server
Is there any workaround available until the issue is fixed?
Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!
